description: Strike or shake a 'teli' and its hollow body gives a resonant tone. The sound may call a gathering, mark an event, warn people, or invite attention; 'kirowi' (signal) names the information it carries rather than the bell itself.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the upper teeth for voiceless dental 't', then release into stressed mid-front 'e'. Move into alveolar lateral 'l' and finish with high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The crisp dental release followed by light 'li' gives 'teli' a compact, bright contour that can recall a bell's first clear note.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A single bell tone can interrupt habit and return attention to the sound occurring now.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A bell's carrying tone lets one small object call a dispersed community together without a powered network.

semantic_domains.communication: an audible object used to call, warn, or mark an event
semantic_domains.creation: a made resonant instrument sounded by contact
semantic_domains.ritual: a bell used to open, close, or gather attention around an observance
